title: mcp_server
description: An MCP transport endpoint exposing tools, resources, or prompts (docs/ontology/README.md §1.1, core entity 'mcp_server'). Non-equivalence rule from the same table: a connector/app may use MCP internally but has different auth and governance. This file is the machine-readable concept declaration internal/ontology loads; it doubles as a JSON Schema for the canonical fields of one normalized MCP server record, plus x- extension keys carrying the logical-identity rule and allowed merge operators from docs/ontology/README.md §3.1/§3.2.

id.description: Stable logical MCP server ID (docs/ontology/README.md §6.2: Codex merges '[mcp_servers.<id>]' by server ID).

tools.description: Discovered tool names, for duplicate logical-capability detection (docs/architecture/reporting.md §8, 'Duplicate capability').

x-nonEquivalenceRule: A connector/app may use MCP internally but has different auth and governance.

x-logicalIdentity.rule: Two physical representations are the same logical MCP server only when both their transport and server ID match. Merging happens by server ID within one transport (docs/ontology/README.md §6.2: Codex 'Merge by server ID following config precedence'); a stdio server and an http server sharing the same id string are not assumed to be the same logical server, and a same-brand connector/app is never folded into this identity even if it wraps MCP internally (docs/ontology/README.md §8 Safety Invariants: 'Same-brand connector, MCP server, plugin tool, and built-in tool are separate sources.').
